questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, how many planetary deities make up the group known as the Navagraha?
questions.explanation: The word Navagraha means 'nine grahas', the nine celestial influencers of Hindu astrology.

questions.q: Which of the Navagraha is the Sun deity of Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Surya is the Sun god, the first and foremost of the nine Navagraha.

questions.q: Which of the Navagraha is the Moon deity in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Chandra is the Moon god, counted among the nine Navagraha.

questions.q: Among the Navagraha, which graha is associated with the planet Mars?
questions.explanation: Mangala (also called Angaraka or Kuja) is the graha associated with the planet Mars.

questions.q: Which graha among the Navagraha corresponds to the planet Mercury?
questions.explanation: Budha is the graha identified with the planet Mercury.

questions.q: In the Navagraha, which deity is the guru of the gods and corresponds to the planet Jupiter?
questions.explanation: Brihaspati, preceptor of the devas, is the graha associated with the planet Jupiter.

questions.q: Which graha among the Navagraha is identified with the planet Venus?
questions.explanation: Shukra, preceptor of the asuras, is the graha associated with the planet Venus.

questions.q: Which of the Navagraha is the deity of the planet Saturn, known for governing discipline and karma?
questions.explanation: Shani is the graha of Saturn, widely propitiated to soften hardship and the fruits of karma.

questions.q: Among the Navagraha, which two are the shadow-grahas that are not physical planets?
questions.explanation: Rahu and Ketu are the shadow-grahas, representing the lunar nodes rather than visible planets.

questions.q: For what purpose are the Navagraha commonly propitiated together in Hindu temples?
questions.explanation: The nine grahas are worshipped collectively to ward off misfortune and ill effects on one's destiny.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which serpent-demon's severed head became the graha Rahu after he drank the nectar of immortality?
questions.explanation: The asura Svarbhanu, decapitated by Vishnu, became Rahu (the head) and Ketu (the body) among the grahas.

questions.q: In the legend of the Navagraha, which two grahas exposed the disguised asura who stole the amrita, leading to his beheading?
questions.explanation: Surya (Sun) and Chandra (Moon) revealed Svarbhanu's disguise, which is why Rahu eternally pursues them, causing eclipses.

questions.q: Which weekday of the Hindu week is named for and ruled by Surya, the Sun graha?
questions.explanation: Sunday (Ravivara) is governed by Surya, the Sun, the first of the seven planetary weekdays.

questions.q: According to tradition, which graha rules Saturday, the weekday associated with discipline and patience?
questions.explanation: Saturday (Shanivara) is ruled by Shani, the graha of Saturn.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography, what vahana (mount or vehicle) is the Sun graha Surya most often depicted riding across the sky?
options: A chariot drawn by seven horses
questions.explanation: Surya is depicted riding a chariot drawn by seven horses, often guided by his charioteer Aruna.

questions.q: According to Hindu lore, who is the father of Shani, the graha of Saturn?
questions.explanation: Shani is traditionally regarded as a son of Surya (the Sun) and his wife Chhaya.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which graha is honoured as the preceptor (guru) of the asuras and master of the science of revival?
questions.explanation: Shukra is the guru of the asuras and is said to possess the Sanjivani knowledge of reviving the dead.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which graha serves as the divine preceptor and counsellor of the devas?
questions.explanation: Brihaspati is the guru of the devas, embodying wisdom and counsel, and rules the planet Jupiter.

questions.q: Which celestial phenomenon do the Puranas attribute to Rahu and Ketu swallowing the Sun or Moon?
questions.explanation: Eclipses are explained as Rahu (or Ketu) seizing the Sun or Moon in revenge for exposing the asura at the churning of the ocean.

questions.q: In which great cosmic episode of the Puranas does the legend of Rahu and Ketu's origin take place?
options: The Samudra Manthana (churning of the ocean)
questions.explanation: Rahu and Ketu arose during the Samudra Manthana, when the asura Svarbhanu stole the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: Which day of the week is traditionally ruled by Chandra, the Moon graha?
questions.explanation: Monday (Somavara) takes its name from Soma, another name for Chandra, the Moon.

questions.q: In the Navagraha, which graha is regarded as the son of Chandra (the Moon) and rules the planet Mercury?
questions.explanation: Budha, the graha of Mercury, is described in the Puranas as the son of Chandra.

questions.q: In Navagraha iconography, on which color body and mount is the fierce graha Mangala (Mars) typically depicted?
options: A white body riding a swan
A blue body riding a vulture
A golden body riding a lion
A red body riding a ram
questions.explanation: Mangala is depicted with a red complexion, fitting Mars, and is often shown riding a ram.

questions.q: According to Hindu tradition, which creature is named as a vahana commonly associated with Shani, the graha of Saturn?
questions.explanation: Shani is traditionally associated with dark mounts such as the crow or vulture, in keeping with his somber nature.

questions.q: In Navagraha temple arrangements, the grahas are placed in a square grid in which the central, governing position is occupied by which deity?
questions.explanation: In the traditional Navagraha shrine layout, Surya the Sun stands at the center with the other eight arranged around him.

questions.q: In Hindu astrology, the dreaded period when Shani transits the houses around one's moon sign is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Sade Sati is the roughly seven-and-a-half-year transit of Shani held to bring trials, prompting his propitiation.

questions.q: Among the Navagraha, Ketu is iconographically represented with the body of which creature, reflecting his origin from the severed asura?
questions.explanation: Ketu, formed from the lower body of Svarbhanu, is depicted as the serpent's tail, while Rahu is the head.

questions.q: In Navagraha iconography, which golden-hued graha, guru of the devas, is most often shown riding a chariot drawn by horses and holding a rosary and book?
questions.explanation: Brihaspati, the Jupiter graha and preceptor of the devas, is depicted golden-hued, riding a chariot drawn by horses and bearing a rosary and a book of sacred knowledge.

questions.q: In Hindu astronomy, which of the Navagraha is identified with the ascending (north) lunar node, the point where the Moon's path crosses the ecliptic going north?
questions.explanation: Rahu is the chaya-graha associated with the ascending (north) lunar node, while Ketu corresponds to the descending (south) node.

questions.q: In the Surya iconography of the Navagraha, which deity is named as the charioteer who drives the Sun's chariot and is depicted without lower limbs?
questions.explanation: Aruna, the elder brother of Garuda, serves as the charioteer of Surya and is described as having no lower limbs.
